fixer.summary: Binary operators should be surrounded by space as configured.
Braces must be placed as configured.

source.code:
<?php

$foo = new class { };

$bar = new class {
    public function test() {
        return true;
    }
};

pint.output:
<?php

$foo = new class
{
};

$bar = new class
{
    public function test()
    {
        return true;
    }
};

php_cs_fixer.output:
<?php

$foo = new class { };

$bar = new class {
    public function test()
    {
        return true;
    }
};

fixer.summary: Whitespace around the keywords of a class, trait, enum or interfaces definition should be one space.

source.code:
<?php

$obj = new class(
    $bar,
    $baz
) extends Base {};

pint.output:
<?php

$obj = new class($bar, $baz) extends Base {};

php_cs_fixer.output:
<?php

$obj = new class (
    $bar,
    $baz
) extends Base {};

fixer.summary: In method arguments and method call, there MUST NOT be a space before each comma and there MUST be one space after each comma. Argument lists MAY be split across multiple lines, where each subsequent line is indented once. When doing so, the first item in the list MUST be on the next line, and there MUST be only one argument per line.
fixer.description: This fixer covers rules defined in PSR2 ¶4.4, ¶4.6.

source.code:
<?php
function sample($a=10,
    $b=20,$c=30) {}
sample(1,
    2);

pint.output:
<?php
function sample($a=10,
    $b=20, $c=30) {}
sample(1,
    2);

php_cs_fixer.output:
<?php
function sample(
    $a=10,
    $b=20,
    $c=30
) {}
sample(
    1,
    2
);

fixer.summary: Classes, constants, properties, and methods MUST have visibility declared, and keyword modifiers MUST be in the following order: inheritance modifier (`abstract` or `final`), visibility modifier (`public`, `protected`, or `private`), set-visibility modifier (`public(set)`, `protected(set)`, or `private(set)`), scope modifier (`static`), mutation modifier (`readonly`), type declaration, name.

source.code:
<?php

abstract class ClassName
{
    const SAMPLE = 1;

    static protected int $beep;

    function zex() {}
}

pint.output:
<?php

abstract class ClassName
{
    const SAMPLE = 1;

    protected static int $beep;

    public function zex() {}
}

php_cs_fixer.output:
<?php

abstract class ClassName
{
    public const SAMPLE = 1;

    protected static int $beep;

    public function zex() {}
}

fixer.summary: All instances created with `new` keyword must (not) be followed by parentheses.

source.code:
<?php

$y = new class() {};

$x = new class {};

pint.output:
<?php

$y = new class {};

$x = new class {};

php_cs_fixer.output:
<?php

$y = new class() {};

$x = new class() {};

fixer.summary: Removes extra blank lines and/or blank lines following configuration.

source.code:
<?php

use Foo;

use Bar;


$baz = "baz";

pint.output:
<?php

use Foo;
use Bar;

$baz = "baz";

php_cs_fixer.output:
<?php

use Foo;
use Bar;


$baz = "baz";

fixer.summary: Ordering `use` statements.

source.code:
<?php
use const BBB;
use const AAAA;

use Acme;
use AAC;
use Bar;

use function DDD;
use function CCC\AA;

pint.output:
<?php
use const AAAA;
use const BBB;

use AAC;
use Acme;
use Bar;

use function CCC\AA;
use function DDD;

php_cs_fixer.output:
<?php
use Acme;
use AAC;

use Bar;
use function DDD;
use function CCC\AA;

use const BBB;
use const AAAA;

fixer.summary: There MUST NOT be more than one property or constant declared per statement.

source.code:
<?php
final class Example
{
    const FOO_1 = 1, FOO_2 = 2;

    private static $bar1 = array(1,2,3), $bar2 = [1,2,3];
}

pint.output:
<?php
final class Example
{
    const FOO_1 = 1;
    const FOO_2 = 2;

    private static $bar1 = array(1,2,3);
    private static $bar2 = [1,2,3];
}

php_cs_fixer.output:
<?php
final class Example
{
    const FOO_1 = 1, FOO_2 = 2;

    private static $bar1 = array(1,2,3);
    private static $bar2 = [1,2,3];
}

fixer.summary: There MUST be one use keyword per declaration.

source.code:
<?php
use Space\Models\ {
    TestModelA,
    TestModelB,
    TestModel,
};

pint.output:
<?php
use Space\Models\TestModelA;
use Space\Models\TestModelB;
use Space\Models\TestModel;

php_cs_fixer.output:
<?php
use Space\Models\ {
    TestModelA,
    TestModelB,
    TestModel,
};

fixer.summary: Ensures a single space after language constructs.

source.code:
<?php

function foo() { yield  from  baz(); }

if ($foo) {
}
   elseif ($bar) {
}

pint.output:
<?php

function foo() { yield from baz(); }

if ($foo) {
}
   elseif ($bar) {
}

php_cs_fixer.output:
<?php

function foo() { yield from  baz(); }

if ($foo) {
} elseif ($bar) {
}

fixer.summary: Unary operators should be placed adjacent to their operands.

source.code:
<?php
$sample ++;

function foo($a, ...   $b) {}

pint.output:
<?php
$sample++;

function foo($a, ...$b) {}

php_cs_fixer.output:
<?php
$sample++;

function foo($a, ...   $b) {}

fixer.summary: There must be a comment when fall-through is intentional in a non-empty case body.
fixer.description: Adds a "no break" comment before fall-through cases, and removes it if there is no fall-through.

source.code:
<?php
switch ($foo) {
    case 1:
        foo();
    case 2:
        bar();
        // no break
        break;
    case 3:
        baz();
}

pint.output:
<?php
switch ($foo) {
    case 1:
        foo();
    case 2:
        bar();
        // no break
        break;
    case 3:
        baz();
}

php_cs_fixer.output:
<?php
switch ($foo) {
    case 1:
        foo();
        // no break
    case 2:
        bar();
        break;
    case 3:
        baz();
}

fixer.summary: Each trait `use` must be done as single statement.

source.code:
<?php
final class Example
{
    use Foo, Bar;
}

pint.output:
<?php
final class Example
{
    use Foo, Bar;
}

php_cs_fixer.output:
<?php
final class Example
{
    use Foo;use Bar;
}

fixer.summary: An empty line feed must precede any configured statement.

source.code:
<?php
switch ($foo) {
    case 42:
        $bar->process();
        break;
    case 44:
        break;
}

pint.output:
<?php
switch ($foo) {
    case 42:
        $bar->process();
        break;
    case 44:
        break;
}

php_cs_fixer.output:
<?php
switch ($foo) {
    case 42:
        $bar->process();

        break;
    case 44:
        break;
}

fixer.summary: Class, trait and interface elements must be separated with one or none blank line.

source.code:
<?php

enum Status: string
{
    case Active = 'active';

    case Inactive = 'inactive';

    case Pending = 'pending';
}

pint.output:
<?php

enum Status: string
{
    case Active = 'active';

    case Inactive = 'inactive';

    case Pending = 'pending';
}

php_cs_fixer.output:
<?php

enum Status: string
{
    case Active = 'active';
    case Inactive = 'inactive';
    case Pending = 'pending';
}

fixer.summary: Removes the leading part of fully qualified symbol references if a given symbol is imported or belongs to the current namespace.

source.code:
<?php

namespace Foo\Test;

class Foo extends \Other\BaseClass
{
    /** @var \Other\PropertyPhpDoc */
    private $array;

    public function __construct(\Other\PropertyPhpDoc $arg) {}
}

pint.output:
<?php

namespace Foo\Test;
use Other\BaseClass;
use Other\PropertyPhpDoc;

class Foo extends BaseClass
{
    /** @var PropertyPhpDoc */
    private $array;

    public function __construct(PropertyPhpDoc $arg) {}
}

php_cs_fixer.output:
<?php

namespace Foo\Test;

class Foo extends \Other\BaseClass
{
    /** @var \Other\PropertyPhpDoc */
    private $array;

    public function __construct(\Other\PropertyPhpDoc $arg) {}
}

fixer.summary: Pre- or post-increment and decrement operators should be used if possible.
Removes `@param`, `@return` and `@var` tags that don't provide any useful information.

source.code:
<?php
class Foo {
    /**
     * @param Bar $bar
     * @param mixed $baz
     */
    public function doFoo(Bar $bar, $baz) {}
}

pint.output:
<?php
class Foo {
    /**
     * @param mixed $baz
     */
    public function doFoo(Bar $bar, $baz) {}
}

php_cs_fixer.output:
<?php
class Foo {
    /**
     */
    public function doFoo(Bar $bar, $baz) {}
}

fixer.summary: Enforce camel (or snake) case for PHPUnit test methods, following configuration.

source.code:
<?php
class MyTest extends \PhpUnit\FrameWork\TestCase
{
    public function testMyCode() {}
}

pint.output:
<?php
class MyTest extends \PhpUnit\FrameWork\TestCase
{
    public function test_my_code() {}
}

php_cs_fixer.output:
<?php
class MyTest extends \PhpUnit\FrameWork\TestCase
{
    public function testMyCode() {}
}

fixer.summary: All items of the given PHPDoc tags must be either left-aligned or (by default) aligned vertically.

source.code:
<?php
/**
 * @param  EngineInterface $templating
 * @param  int  $code       response status
 * @param  mixed    &$reference  
 *
 */

pint.output:
<?php
/**
 * @param  EngineInterface  $templating
 * @param  int  $code  response status
 * @param  mixed  &$reference
 *
 */

php_cs_fixer.output:
<?php
/**
 * @param EngineInterface $templating
 * @param int             $code       response status
 * @param mixed           &$reference
 *
 */

fixer.summary: Annotations in PHPDoc should be ordered in defined sequence.

source.code:
<?php
/**
 * Hello there!
 *
 * @throws Exception|RuntimeException foo
 * @custom Test!
 * @return int  Return the number of changes.
 * @param string $foo
 * @param bool   $bar Bar
 */

pint.output:
<?php
/**
 * Hello there!
 *
 * @custom Test!
 * @param string $foo
 * @param bool   $bar Bar
 * @return int  Return the number of changes.
 * @throws Exception|RuntimeException foo
 */

php_cs_fixer.output:
<?php
/**
 * Hello there!
 *
 * @custom Test!
 * @param string $foo
 * @param bool   $bar Bar
 * @throws Exception|RuntimeException foo
 * @return int  Return the number of changes.
 */

fixer.summary: Annotations in PHPDoc should be grouped together so that annotations of the same type immediately follow each other. Annotations of a different type are separated by a single blank line.

source.code:
<?php
/**
 * @throws Exception|RuntimeException foo
 * @param string $foo
 *
 * @param bool   $bar Bar
 * @return int  Return the number of changes.
 */

pint.output:
<?php
/**
 * @throws Exception|RuntimeException foo
 *
 * @param string $foo
 * @param bool   $bar Bar
 * @return int  Return the number of changes.
 */

php_cs_fixer.output:
<?php
/**
 * @throws Exception|RuntimeException foo
 *
 * @param string $foo
 * @param bool   $bar Bar
 *
 * @return int  Return the number of changes.
 */

fixer.summary: Single-line comments and multi-line comments with only one line of actual content should use the `//` syntax.

source.code:
<?php
/* first comment */
$a = 1;

/*
 * second
 * comment
 */
$b = 2;

# third comment
$c = 3;

pint.output:
<?php
/* first comment */
$a = 1;

/*
 * second
 * comment
 */
$b = 2;

// third comment
$c = 3;

php_cs_fixer.output:
<?php
// first comment
$a = 1;

/*
 * second
 * comment
 */
$b = 2;

// third comment
$c = 3;

fixer.summary: Write conditions in Yoda style (`true`), non-Yoda style (`['equal' => false, 'identical' => false, 'less_and_greater' => false]`) or ignore those conditions (`null`) based on configuration.

source.code:
<?php
    // Yoda style:
    if (null === $foo) {
        echo "null";
    }

    // Non-Yoda style:
    if ($bar === null) {
        echo "null";
    }

pint.output:
<?php
    // Yoda style:
    if ($foo === null) {
        echo "null";
    }

    // Non-Yoda style:
    if ($bar === null) {
        echo "null";
    }

php_cs_fixer.output:
<?php
    // Yoda style:
    if (null === $foo) {
        echo "null";
    }

    // Non-Yoda style:
    if (null === $bar) {
        echo "null";
    }

skipped.reason: No evaluated sample produced different Pint and PHP-CS-Fixer outputs for this rule.
